gender.male: The seafood counter has been occupied since early morning. The counter worker has seen who came through and is uncertain what to do about it.
gender.female: The seafood counter is cold and busy. You lean in and ask the question quietly. The response tells you more than you expected.

Seating Area.default: Chairs and common space where people wait and watch. The seating area sees more of the building's life than any office.

gender.male: You take a seat and let the room run. In ten minutes, you know who's been here too long and who's waiting for the wrong thing.
gender.female: The seating area is full of people in transit. You read the room the way you read case files — quickly and without missing the margin notes.

Security.default: Uniformed presence and institutional watchfulness. The security team sees everything the cameras don't and forgets nothing convenient to remember.

gender.male: You badge in to the security supervisor. Professional courtesy between people who know how buildings work — and how they don't.
gender.female: You approach the security desk. The officer sizes you up. You let them finish and then ask for the incident log.

Security Camera.default: A single eye mounted at an angle that shows more than it's supposed to. The security camera is the city's most honest narrator.

gender.male: A single eye mounted at an angle that covers more than it seems to. The security camera is the building's most patient observer.
gender.female: The camera position covers the approach and the entry. What it sees, it keeps. Quiet, steady, and indifferent.

Security Cameras.default: A network of eyes covering every significant surface in the building. The camera system records more than anyone has time to review.

gender.male: The security camera grid map has three dead zones that were never patched. Those zones are the starting point.
gender.female: The camera system runs around the clock. What it captures is stored, filed, and mostly ignored until it matters.

Security Checkpoint.default: A controlled access point where everyone is scanned and logged. The checkpoint knows who came in. You want to know who didn't come out.

gender.male: The security checkpoint credential log records every pass through the point. A double-check of credentials by the officer on duty generates a secondary entry in that record.
gender.female: The security checkpoint log shows an entry without a corresponding exit. The interior camera coverage is the next question.

Security Desk.default: The ground-floor command post of building security. The desk officer has seen everything from this chair. You ask them what they remember about the night in question.

gender.male: You lean on the security desk. The guard knows trouble walks on two feet. You flash your badge and ask for the visitor log.
gender.female: The security desk officer is alert and practiced. She remembers the visitor from Wednesday without being prompted. She's been waiting to tell someone.

Security Office.default: The operational center of a building's safety infrastructure. The security office holds incident reports, daily logs, and the quiet authority of people who watch screens all day.

gender.male: You sit down in the security office and start from the top. Camera grid, access logs, incident reports. You'll be here a while.
gender.female: The security office is organised and current. The chief officer runs a tight operation. She knows the building better than anyone in it.

Security Operations.default: The active monitoring center for a large property — screens, analysts, and the ongoing business of watching for what shouldn't be there.

gender.male: Security operations is staffed around the clock. You arrive at shift change deliberately. The outgoing team remembers more than the incoming one has been told.
gender.female: You enter security operations with credentials and questions. The analyst on duty pulls up the timeline before you can ask. She already connected the dots.

Security Patrol.default: Mobile oversight of a defined territory. The patrol officer walks the same route at the same time unless something makes them deviate. Find the deviation.

gender.male: The patrol log records every route deviation with a time and officer identifier. Route variations outside the standard pattern are a distinct category of entry in the patrol record.
gender.female: The patrol officer's route is documented to the minute. You find the minute where it changed and trace it forward.

Security System.default: Integrated alarms, cameras, and sensors woven into the building's bones. The security system knows what happened. You just need to speak its language.

gender.male: You connect to the security system dashboard. Motion logs, alarm triggers, manual overrides. One override in the middle of the night. That's your start.
gender.female: The security system export takes three minutes to run. You use the time to write down the questions you'll need answered by the end of it.

Self-Checkout.default: Automated checkout lanes that eliminate the human element — or so people assume. The machines remember every transaction.

gender.male: A malfunctioning camera above a self-checkout lane doesn't affect the card transaction record. The card log and the camera record are independent systems.
gender.female: The self-checkout terminal log carries cash and card transactions differently. Card transactions leave enough.

Self-Order Kiosk.default: A touchscreen that takes the order and removes the person from the transaction. Efficient, trackable, and indifferent to alibis.

gender.male: Self-order kiosk. Whoever placed the order late in the evening used a loyalty account they stopped using after that night.
gender.female: You query the self-order system. The loyalty data tells you what they ordered, when they were here, and where they sat.

Self-Service Kiosk.default: A standalone transaction terminal that does what a person used to do. The kiosk doesn't editorialize. It records.

gender.male: The kiosk transaction history shows two visits at the same kiosk and the same hour under two different names.
gender.female: The self-service kiosk log shows a mid-process cancellation. The camera angle covers the point where the transaction stopped.

Senior Center.default: A community space for an age that the city sometimes forgets it produced. The senior center is full of people with nothing left to prove and everything worth saying.

gender.male: You sit down with the senior center regulars. They remember everything. They just needed someone to ask.
gender.female: The senior center is warm and busy. You introduce yourself and ask your question. The room knows the answer before anyone says it aloud.

Server Room.default: Cold air and server hum — the infrastructure of everything digital in this building. The server room is where data lives until someone decides it shouldn't.

gender.male: The server room hums with the constant work of machines that never sleep. Cool air, blinking lights, and the sound of information in motion.
gender.female: You stand in the server room with your IT forensics contact. Cold air, live drives, and a deletion event from a recent overnight window.

Server Rooms.default: Multiple data infrastructure spaces, each one a layer of the operation's digital nervous system.

gender.male: You work through the server rooms with a floor plan. One room is off the schematic. You find it on the third pass.
gender.female: The server rooms are catalogued but not all of them match the building permit drawings. You start with the one that doesn't.

Service Bays.default: Working spaces designed for maintenance and repair. The service bays know what's been brought in, what's been worked on, and what left differently than it arrived.

gender.male: You walk the service bays. The job ticket for bay four is marked complete but the vehicle in it hasn't moved.
gender.female: The service bay log shows an after-hours entry that nobody flagged. You find the technician who was on duty and didn't log the work.

Service Center.default: A centralized support operation handling the building's or business's ongoing needs. The service center touches every department.

gender.male: You go to the service center with a specific request and a broader agenda. The rep handles the first. You handle the second.
gender.female: The service center documentation is complete for every request except one. The undocumented one is the notable one.

Shampoo Bowls.default: Tilted chairs and running water for hair washing — where people close their eyes and talk. The shampoo bowl is an accidental confessional.

gender.male: You wait near the shampoo area. The stylist is mid-rinse and mid-conversation. The conversation is more useful to you.
gender.female: You take a chair near the shampoo bowls and listen. Clients talk freely here. One of them said something recently that hasn't left the stylist's mind.

Shared Parking.default: A lot used by multiple tenants on a rotating schedule. Shared parking is jurisdictionally ambiguous and photographically rich.

gender.male: Three different tenant cameras cover the shared lot from three different angles. Each covers what the others miss.
gender.female: Shared parking means shared responsibility and blurred accountability. That's usually where you find what you're looking for.

Shipping Bay.default: An industrial loading area for outbound freight. The shipping bay is the last checkpoint before something leaves your reach.

gender.male: You arrive at the shipping bay as the last truck of the day is staged. You stop the driver before they pull out.
gender.female: The shipping bay outbound log has a manifest discrepancy. You find the dock supervisor and make them walk you through it.

Shipping Station.default: A dedicated station for processing outbound shipments. Every package labeled here has a destination. You follow one.

gender.male: The shipping station scales log weight by session. One package in the period in question doesn't add up.
gender.female: The shipping station processes outbound packages with barcodes, weight checks, and manifests. Every parcel leaves with a number and a destination.

Shopping Cart Corral.default: A metal cage at the edge of a parking lot. The cart corral is a transition zone where the transaction ends and decisions get made.

gender.male: The shopping cart corral is near the east entrance camera blind spot. That's not a coincidence.
gender.female: The cart corral shows scuff marks near the base that don't match standard return activity.

Shower Facilities.default: A communal washing area in a building that expects people to work hard enough to need one. The shower facilities are honest about what this place costs people.

gender.male: The shower facility is tiled and industrial. Steam and the smell of institutional soap. The drain gurgles with the patience of plumbing.
gender.female: You clear the shower facilities with a female officer. The drain grate was disturbed recently. You have it processed.

Showroom.default: A curated environment built to sell a vision. The showroom is all surface. You look for what's underneath.

gender.male: The showroom floor is polished and persuasive. You ignore the presentation and look at the back corners and the room behind the display.
gender.female: You walk the showroom at your own pace. The sales associate follows. You let them talk and listen for what they don't say.

Shuttle Service.default: A managed transport loop connecting this location to somewhere else. The shuttle knows exactly who boarded and when.

gender.male: You speak to the shuttle driver. They run the same route twelve hours a day and remember faces. Yours included, now.
gender.female: The shuttle service log is a digital record of every boarding event. Each run generates a timestamped manifest of passengers and their boarding points.

Signage Pylon.default: A tall road-side sign announcing the property's identity. The pylon sees the street. Everything on the street sees the pylon.

gender.male: You stand at the signage pylon and survey the sightlines. Whoever was watching the entrance had the best angle from here.
gender.female: The pylon base is accessible from the service road. Someone used that access recently. The ground shows it.

Sky Lobby.default: An elevated lobby midway up a tall building where express and local elevators converge. The sky lobby is a choke point and an observation deck.

gender.male: The sky lobby access records show an entry at a specific time. The exit is not recorded.
gender.female: The sky lobby is glass and altitude. You take a position near the elevator bank and watch who's watching.

Sky Lounge.default: A premium lounge above the city's competing noise. The sky lounge is for the kind of people who conduct their business where nobody else can overhear — or so they think.

gender.male: You ride the express elevator to the sky lounge. The sommelier recognizes you. You've been here before, under different circumstances.
gender.female: The sky lounge operates on reservation and appearance. You arrive without either and let your badge handle the introductions.

Snack Bar.default: Packaged food and cold drinks dispensed at a markup. The snack bar is where the city goes when it doesn't have time for a real meal. Which is always.

gender.male: You grab a coffee at the snack bar. Bad coffee, but it's hot. The attendant has been here all day. They've seen things.
gender.female: The snack bar is between the elevator bank and the exit. Everyone passes through. You take a position and wait.

Social Hall.default: A large communal gathering space used for events, meals, and the kind of informal conversations that shape communities. Whoever runs the social hall runs the room.

gender.male: The social hall is set for a function that won't happen tonight. Not after what you found.
gender.female: You walk the social hall after the event. Folding chairs and leftover programs. The event coordinator remembers everyone who attended.

Soda Fountain.default: An old-fashioned counter service feature. The soda fountain slows people down, which is exactly the kind of pace where conversations happen.

gender.male: You take a stool at the soda fountain. The server has been behind this counter long enough to know every regular on sight. They know the regulars.
gender.female: You order something cold at the soda fountain and let the counterperson talk. They always do eventually.

Sommelier Station.default: A professional wine service post where expertise meets occasion. The sommelier knows who orders what and who they order it with.

gender.male: You speak to the sommelier. He remembers the table in the corner, the bottle they selected, and the conversation that was louder than the guests intended.
gender.female: The sommelier is meticulous and observant. She describes the table, the guests, and the moment when the mood shifted — unprompted.

Sound System.default: Speakers and amplifiers built to fill a space with managed audio. The sound system also picks up what it isn't supposed to when the wrong settings are active.

gender.male: The AV tech's answer to a question about the recording buffer takes a long moment to arrive.
gender.female: A venue sound system with ambient capture capability maintains a settings log. Active capture parameters are stored as part of the event record.

Spa.default: A space of deliberate calm layered over whatever brought the client here. The spa staff see people in their most unguarded state.

gender.male: You check in with the spa manager. Appointment logs, treatment records, and a client who left without completing a session.
gender.female: The esthetician remembers specific clients with clarity. A recent client's demeanour was notable — the controlled environment didn't help.

Special Ed Room.default: A specialized classroom built for individualized instruction. The level of care in here reflects the people who designed it and the staff who run it.

gender.male: You speak quietly with the special ed teacher. They know their students and their families intimately. They share what's relevant with practiced discretion.
gender.female: The special education room is thoughtfully arranged. The teacher has been watching the door since you arrived. She tells you what she knows.

Speed Bumps.default: Asphalt ridges designed to slow vehicles down. Speed bumps mark the boundary between moving freely and moving carefully. You note who ignored them.

gender.male: The tire tracks over the speed bumps tell you the vehicle was moving fast. The time that damage was done tells you why.
gender.female: Fresh scrape marks on the speed bump show a vehicle cleared it at speed recently. The timestamp is on the camera.

Spin Room.default: A cycling studio of synchronised effort and loud music. The spin room is thirty people suffering together and calling it progress.

gender.male: The spin studio runs on a booking system that tracks every reserved slot. Each session has a registered occupant and a corresponding attendance record.
gender.female: The spin studio instructor tracks attendance against bookings as a standard class management practice. A booked spot that goes unoccupied for a session is noted in the attendance record.

Sports Bar.default: Multiple screens, loud opinions, and the kind of crowd that makes observation difficult but eavesdropping easy.

gender.male: You claim a corner stool at the sports bar. Loud, bright, anonymous. Your kind of working environment when you need to listen.
gender.female: The sports bar is the kind of loud that makes people feel invisible. You use that. The conversation two stools over is what you came for.

Staff Lounge.default: The room where employees are briefly off-duty. The staff lounge is where the real institutional knowledge lives — in the conversations nobody thinks to record.

gender.male: You enter the staff lounge during the overlap break. The workers are tired and candid. You ask your question and listen.
gender.female: The staff lounge is where the real building operates. You make coffee, take a seat, and let the room decide you're safe to talk to.

Staff Room.default: An employee-only space holding the accumulated detritus of daily work. The staff room tells you how the people here actually feel about their jobs.

gender.male: The staff room bulletin board is a newspaper of grievances and schedules. You read it carefully.
gender.female: You step into the staff room while the supervisor is occupied. The whiteboard and the table both have things to tell you.

Stage.default: An elevated platform designed for performance and attention. The stage is where people become what they need to be seen as.

gender.male: You stand on the stage in the empty hall. The sightlines from up here cut both ways.
gender.female: The stage was occupied last night. You note the position of the lighting rig and what it would have illuminated — and what it would have hidden.

Stairwell.default: Concrete and metal between floors — utilitarian and overlooked. The stairwell is where the city's off-the-record movement happens.

gender.female: You descend the stairwell with your hand on your jacket. The acoustics carry sound from two floors up. You stop and listen.

Stamp Vending.default: Automated postage dispensers for physical correspondence. In an era of digital communication, a stamp purchase is a deliberate act with a paper record on both ends.

gender.male: The stamp vending machine's last activity was at an unusual hour. The transaction record is retrievable.
gender.female: The stamp machine records every transaction with a time and a denomination. Physical correspondence begins with the stamp purchase and ends with a dated postmark.

gender.female: The storage room is the building's repository of things nobody handles daily. That's why what you're looking for is here.

gender.male: The storage closet was accessed multiple times recently by a credential that shouldn't have been active. You get the door open and start documenting.
gender.female: The storage closet's contents are partly expected and partly not. The unexpected portion is documented.

Storage Lot.default: An outdoor area for oversized or overflow items. The storage lot operates at the margins of the facility's attention. Things get lost in margins.

gender.female: The storage lot sits at the back of the property, outside direct camera coverage. The positioning is deliberate.

Storage Room.default: A utility space that holds more than inventory. Storage rooms are archives, repositories, and occasionally the answer to the question you couldn't place.

gender.female: You move through the storage room systematically. The disorganization is cultivated — designed to make things hard to find. You find them anyway.

Storage Units.default: Individual locked compartments rented by the city's people to hold what they can't keep at home. What's stored here is personal, guarded, and occasionally incriminating.

gender.male: The storage unit sits behind a rolling door and a padlock. The rental agreement is on file at the front desk, along with every other unit's history.
gender.female: The storage unit door rolls up slowly. Whatever is inside was placed here with care. You document everything before you touch anything.

gender.female: The storm drain access hatch shows recent disturbance. You call for an environmental forensics team before proceeding.

Student Center.default: The hub of campus social life, where ambition and exhaustion share a common space. Everyone passes through here eventually.

gender.female: You move through the student center with your credentials visible. Students make room. One of them looks like they've been waiting for someone.

Study Rooms.default: Bookable quiet spaces for focused work. The study room reservation system is a detailed record of who was where and when.

gender.male: The study room booking log shows a reservation held for three hours with a departure forty-five minutes in.
gender.female: A study room booking system logs credentials against reservations. An inactive credential used to make a reservation creates an anomaly in the booking record.

gender.male: You take a seat near the styling stations and let the ambient conversation work on your behalf.
gender.female: You introduce yourself to the lead stylist. She's been doing this long enough to know what's worth mentioning. She mentions it.

Sunday School Rooms.default: Classrooms attached to a faith community where the youngest members are taught to understand the world. Someone with access to these rooms used that access after hours.

Supervisor Office.default: The authority at the center of an operation's daily running. The supervisor office sees the whole floor and knows where the problems live.

gender.female: The supervisor reviews your credentials carefully. She's protective of her team. You respect that. You ask your questions accordingly.

Surgery Center.default: A facility where precision is mandatory and every event is documented. The surgery center holds records of what was done — and occasionally what was undone.

Tasting Room.default: A curated experience of flavors and decisions. The tasting room is where business happens over small pours and large implications.

gender.female: The tasting room is designed to relax its guests. A relaxed proprietor describes things they might not otherwise share.

gender.female: The teacher lounge is quiet between classes. A veteran teacher pours you a coffee before you even ask. She's ready to talk.

gender.female: You speak with the teaching facility coordinator. She runs a tight schedule and knows when something doesn't fit it.

gender.female: The teen zone coordinator knows her regulars. She tells you which one hasn't been coming in. You ask why they might have stopped.

gender.female: The toddler room is staffed with careful intention. You speak to the caregiver quietly and keep your questions to what's necessary.

gender.female: The tool crib attendant runs a tight inventory. She noticed the discrepancy before you asked about it. She kept her own notes.

gender.female: The towel service log shows an issuance that wasn't tied to an active room booking. The record is specific about time and quantity.

Track.default: A measured circuit for repetitive physical effort. The track is where consistency is built and endurance is tested. Someone's pattern broke on this track.

Training Center.default: A facility for developing capability — physical, professional, or operational. The training center shapes how people respond under pressure. You look for who didn't apply the training.

gender.male: The training center completion records show full module passage up to a recent date. The competence isn't in question. The behaviour after is.
gender.female: The training center director has documentation on every candidate. She pulls the file before you ask. She had a feeling.

Training Facility.default: A dedicated space built for the ongoing development of personnel or athletes. The training facility creates a paper trail of performance, attendance, and deviation.

gender.male: You work through the training facility access logs. The credential used to enter at an unusual hour belongs to someone whose authorisation ended not long ago.
gender.female: The training facility supervisor runs a disciplined operation. She knows which records you need and what they mean.

Training Room.default: A room reserved for developing skill under observation. The training room is where people are watched closely. Not all of them know by whom.

gender.male: The training room was accessed after hours by a user credential that expired last quarter. You find out how it was still active.
gender.female: The training room booking and session log cover scheduled and unscheduled use independently. A session that doesn't appear in the booking system is still visible in the room's access record.

Transit Hub.default: A convergence point for multiple transportation modes. The transit hub moves thousands of people daily. It's the city's largest unscripted theater.

gender.male: The transit hub is all motion and ambient noise. You fix on one face in the crowd and follow it to its destination.
gender.female: You stand at the center of the transit hub. Hundreds of people, dozens of trajectories. You isolate the one you came for.

Transportation Service.default: A managed transit operation running on schedule and obligation. The transportation service records every trip, every driver, every destination.

gender.male: The transportation service manifest shows a documented pickup request and a documented destination. The destination is a next step.
gender.female: Digital dispatch logs for a transportation service are timestamped records of every completed and requested trip. The records are maintained for the standard operational retention period.

Trash Chute.default: A vertical disposal shaft that the building's occupants use to eliminate what they don't want. The trash chute swallows everything without judgement.

gender.male: The trash chute collection for the previous night is still in the compactor. The overnight window is before the pickup.
gender.female: What was disposed of through the trash chute is recoverable before the scheduled collection. The disposal record exists.

Trash Enclosure.default: A fenced or walled area containing the building's refuse containers. The trash enclosure is where things go when people think no one is watching.

gender.male: The trash enclosure padlock and the replacement record are both maintained by the property management office. Lock changes are documented with a date and a reason code.
gender.female: The trash enclosure is the last stop before disposal. What's in there tells you what the building consumed and discarded.

Trauma Center.default: A high-acuity medical unit built for the city's worst moments. The trauma center is where the city's violence arrives with a heartbeat and leaves with a record.

gender.male: You stand in the trauma center corridor with your badge out and your voice low. The charge nurse is already looking for you.
gender.female: The trauma center never stops. You navigate it with purpose and a clear ID. The attending physician meets you in the hallway.

Travel Services.default: A desk that handles the logistics of getting somewhere else. Travel services know where people were going — and when they stopped buying tickets.

gender.male: The travel services booking history includes a one-way ticket purchased recently. The record is current.
gender.female: The travel services agent is cooperative and precise. She shows you every reservation made and cancelled in the relevant period.

Triage Area.default: The front line of emergency care, where severity is sorted and priority is assigned. The triage area is the city's most honest waiting room.

gender.male: You work through the triage area with your warrant. The charge nurse knows why you're here and where the records are.
gender.female: The triage area is controlled chaos. You move through it with purpose. The information you need is in the intake log from the night in question.

Truck Bays.default: Oversized loading and unloading stations for heavy freight. The truck bays handle what the rest of the building can't. That includes things that don't appear on any manifest.

gender.male: You walk the truck bays during the shift change. The driver from the last overnight run is still in the yard. You find them before they leave.
gender.female: The truck bays handle deliveries on a tight schedule. Dock assignments, arrival times, and departure windows run the rhythm of the loading area.

UPS Room.default: Uninterruptible power supply infrastructure keeping the building alive when the city's grid fails. The UPS room log records every power event. Gaps in continuity are meaningful.

gender.male: The UPS room recorded a power interruption during the night. That window killed the cameras on an upper floor long enough to matter.
gender.female: The UPS power log shows a deliberate load reduction event that produced a seven-minute camera blackout. The event was planned.

Underground Parking.default: Below-grade vehicle storage where lighting is managed and echoes carry. Underground parking is where the city puts what it doesn't want to look at.

gender.male: You descend into the underground parking facility. Your footsteps echo. You work the levels from bottom to top.
gender.female: The underground lot is dimly lit by design. You noted the camera blind spots on the building schematic before you came down here.

Underground Tanks.default: Buried fuel or chemical storage operating below the visible surface of a property. Underground tanks are regulated, monitored, and occasionally the origin of significant problems.

gender.male: The underground tank inspection report was filed some time ago. You find the discrepancy the inspector noted and then didn't follow up on.
gender.female: The environmental compliance documentation for the underground tanks shows signed readings that don't match what the sensor recorded.

Used Car Lot.default: A property of history, repaint, and previous ownership. The used car lot trades in second chances — not all of them legitimate.

gender.male: You walk the used car lot with your VIN scanner. One of these vehicles has a title history that doesn't hold together.
gender.female: A used car lot's sales record tracks every vehicle by date of sale and documentation status. Vehicles that moved with incomplete documentation are a distinct category in the lot's own records.

VIP Entrance.default: A separate access point for people who have arranged not to be seen by anyone they don't choose. The VIP entrance is where the official record ends.

gender.male: The VIP entrance is discreet by design. Separate door, separate corridor, and the kind of quiet that money buys.
gender.female: You approach the VIP entrance with your warrant. Whoever came through here last night didn't use the main door for a reason.

VIP Lanes.default: Expedited access routes for those who paid for priority. VIP lanes create a two-tier system with two different sets of witnesses.

gender.male: The VIP lane attendant's memory for recent sessions is specific. The face from the session in question is described with confidence.
gender.female: The VIP lane access system logs every credential use against the registered holder. The system records both the event and the credential presented.

VIP Lounge.default: A restricted space for premium guests, away from general population and general oversight. The VIP lounge is where things get said that nobody writes down.

gender.male: The VIP lounge opens up. The bartender has a good memory and a professional inclination to share it with the right authority.
gender.female: The VIP lounge staff recognized you as law enforcement before you said a word. Good. They were waiting for someone to ask.

VIP Parking.default: Reserved spaces close to the door, monitored and exclusive. The VIP parking log is a precise record of which vehicles were present for what events.

gender.male: VIP parking is plated and logged. The entries from last night are cross-referenceable against a known list. One plate is on both.
gender.female: The VIP parking attendant has a detailed log and a sharp memory. She describes the vehicle and its driver without prompting.

VIP Seating.default: Premium positions at any event, with better access and higher visibility. Whoever sat in VIP seating last night saw everything. You find them.

gender.male: The VIP seating assignments show a seat adjacent to a point of interest registered under an alias. The name is runnable.
gender.female: The VIP section uses a ticket scanner that generates an entry log for the event period. The scanner records the admission event but not the departure.

Vaccine Station.default: A clinical station for immunization services. The vaccine station logs every patient. Every log is a record of who was here and when.

gender.male: The vaccine station records show a scheduled appointment attended but not completed. The early departure has a note in the file.
gender.female: The vaccine station appointment log shows a cancelled booking after months of consistent attendance. The cancellation timing is notable.

Vacuum Station.default: Industrial or commercial vacuum equipment for vehicle or facility cleaning. The vacuum station is where things get removed — not always just dirt.

gender.male: The vacuum station filter bag was pulled recently. You have the lab process it before the bin is cleared.
gender.female: You arrive at the vacuum station. Industrial hoses and the roar of suction. What goes in doesn't come back out.

Valet Parking.default: Keys handed over and vehicles moved on trust. The valet service touches every car that passes through. They also see who drives them and who they're driving away from.

gender.male: The valet operation logs every vehicle with entry and exit times. A four-hour stay is recorded and timestamped.
gender.female: The valet log records every vehicle handoff with a timestamp and a note field. Observations made by the valet on duty are retained as part of the handoff record.

Valet Service.default: Premium vehicle handling that creates a detailed record of arrivals, departures, and the gap between them.

gender.male: Valet service creates accountability on both ends of a visit. You use that accountability.
gender.female: The valet service documentation is precise. Drop-off times, pick-up times, and the careful choreography of keys and tickets.

Vault.default: The most secured space in any financial institution or high-value property. The vault doesn't care who you are — only whether you have the combination.

gender.male: You stand at the vault door. Heavy steel, mechanical lock, and the kind of silence that comes from six inches of reinforced wall.
gender.female: The vault access requires dual authorization and documentation. You have both. What's inside required someone to override one of them.

Vehicle Storage.default: A secured compound for long-term vehicle holding. What gets stored here is out of circulation — until someone decides it isn't.

gender.male: You locate the vehicle in storage and document its condition before anyone can prepare an explanation.
gender.female: The vehicle storage manifest shows the unit was checked in but not checked out. You verify the vehicle is still there. It isn't.

Vending.default: Automated transaction machines dispensing the city's minor needs. Vending machines are anonymous, widely used, and thoroughly logged.

gender.male: The vending machine near the loading dock was accessed at 2 AM. The product selection was odd. The payment method was odder.
gender.female: The vending machine transaction history for the unit near the third-floor exit shows six uses between midnight and 4 AM.

Vending Machines.default: Rows of automated dispensers representing the full range of commercial human need. What people buy when they're alone tells you something.

gender.male: The vending machine block is across from the security desk. You check which ones were active during the blackout window.
gender.female: The vending machine activity logs show activity during a period when the building was supposed to be empty.

Ventilation.default: Ductwork and airflow managing the building's atmosphere. The ventilation system knows every space it touches. So does anyone with a schematic and a reason.

gender.male: The ventilation layout against the building plans shows an intake for the secured floor running through an unsecured corridor.
gender.female: The ventilation access points are marked in the maintenance plan. One has been opened recently from the wrong side.

Ventilation System.default: Integrated HVAC infrastructure running through every wall and ceiling in the building. It circulates air — and carries sound further than people realize.

gender.male: The ventilation system floor-by-floor inspection reveals a source in the east duct that doesn't belong in this building.
gender.female: The ventilation system map shows a route from the secured server room to the maintenance corridor. It wasn't in the original drawings.

Visitor Center.default: The official face of a building or institution, designed to welcome and manage public access. The visitor center logs who comes in and provides just enough information to keep them satisfied.

gender.male: You sign in at the visitor center and get a badge that underestimates your access. You work with what you have.
gender.female: The visitor center records show a handful of visits in the relevant period. The staff member who assisted both times is still on site.

Wait Station.default: A service staging area in a restaurant or event space. The wait station is the operational nerve center of the front of house — and the place most conversations get overheard.

gender.male: You position yourself near the wait station. The servers consolidate here between runs. So does the conversation about what happened at table seven.
gender.female: You speak with the lead server at the wait station. She has a professional memory for every table's needs and every odd moment of service.

Waiting Area.default: Where the city exercises patience it doesn't always have. The waiting area is a place of tension and observation. You use both.

gender.male: You take a chair in the waiting area and let the room run. In ten minutes you've built a working theory from what you see.
gender.female: The waiting area is full of people who've been here too long. You study them. One of them is waiting for something other than their appointment.

Waiting Room.default: A holding space between arrival and admission. The waiting room is a social contract: you sit, you wait, and eventually someone calls your name.

gender.male: You settle into the waiting room and observe. Old habit. Every room is a scene. Every scene has something worth noting.
gender.female: The waiting room is arranged to feel calm. It isn't. You note who came in after you and positioned themselves near the exit.

Walk-In Closet.default: Personal storage space that reveals more about a person than any interview. The walk-in closet is a biography written in choices.

gender.male: You document the walk-in closet contents methodically. What's missing tells you as much as what's still here.
gender.female: The walk-in closet is organized with precision. The disruption near the back left shelf was recent and deliberate.

Walk-In Cooler.default: Industrial cold storage for food or medical product. The walk-in cooler is a controlled environment — and occasionally an uncontrolled one.

gender.male: The walk-in cooler door was left ajar. The temperature alarm triggered in the early hours. You find out who was inside.
gender.female: The walk-in cooler log shows an after-hours entry without authorisation. The product inventory shows what was touched.

Walk-In Freezer.default: Sub-zero storage where temperature is identity. The walk-in freezer is the coldest point in the operation — in every sense.

gender.male: You pull open the walk-in freezer door. Whatever you expected to find in here, you found something else.
gender.female: The walk-in freezer holds its temperature and its secrets at the same steady minus eighteen. Everything in here is preserved exactly as it was left.

Wash Bay.default: An industrial cleaning station for vehicles or equipment. What gets cleaned in the wash bay leaves traces that detailing was designed to remove.

gender.male: You swab the wash bay drain before the next cleaning cycle runs. Whatever was cleaned here last night left something behind.
gender.female: The wash bay is wet and running. Soap, pressure, and the steady drain of water that carries the day's grime away.

Waxing Room.default: A private treatment room for hair removal services. Clients are alone with their service provider and frequently more candid than they'd be otherwise.

gender.male: You wait outside the waxing room for your appointment to complete. The esthetician comes out knowing more than she expected to.
gender.female: The waxing room esthetician has a long-term client record. The last visit and the conversation during it are both recalled in detail.

Weight Room.default: Iron and rubber and the effort of physical transformation. The weight room is where the body's limits get tested. You note who stopped showing up.

gender.male: The weight room log captures personal records, equipment settings, and session history. Rack configuration left between sessions reflects the last user's session details.
gender.female: The weight room attendance system captures every session by member and time. A consistent usage log reflects the member's training schedule and physical routine.

Welding Shop.default: Sparks and structure — metal joined under controlled heat. The welding shop records what it fabricates. Not everything fabricated is on the log.

gender.male: The welding shop has a job ticket system. One ticket was completed off-schedule and the material used doesn't match the order.
gender.female: The welding shop output log shows a custom fabrication job run after hours with no client name and no purchase order number.

Wellness Room.default: A quiet space for employees or members to recover and decompress. The wellness room is used by people carrying more than they're supposed to. You know the type.

gender.male: The wellness room voluntary sign-in shows multiple recent entries from one individual. The frequency is a pressure indicator.
gender.female: The wellness room coordinator has observed the signs of institutional stress in her clients. A recent pattern of use fits that profile.

Whitening Room.default: A clinical aesthetic room for dental or cosmetic whitening procedures. Close-range work, captive conversation, and a remarkably candid setting.

gender.male: The whitening room appointment log shows an arrival and a departure before the procedure started. The timing is documented.
gender.female: The whitening room technician's record of the appointment covers the conversation as well as the procedure. Both are relevant.

WiFi.default: Wireless network access serving the building's population. The WiFi router logs every device that connects. In the right hands, that's a complete attendance record.

gender.male: The WiFi router logs every device that connects, with timestamps and MAC addresses. Device records are better than visitor logs.
gender.female: The router hums quietly in the corner. Every device that connects leaves a digital handshake. The network remembers what people forget.

WiFi.default: Wireless access point covering the space. Every connection is a timestamp, and timestamps tell their own story.

gender.male: The IT manager keeps the network running with the quiet competence of someone who's seen every kind of outage.
gender.female: A device connection authenticated from outside a building's physical footprint is a network anomaly. The anomaly is recorded in the connection log.

Wine Cellar.default: Temperature-controlled storage for premium bottles and their implicit value. The wine cellar keeps track of what came in, what went out, and what's inexplicably missing.

gender.male: You go down to the wine cellar with the sommelier. Three bottles from a specific case are unaccounted for. That case has significance beyond the label.
gender.female: The wine cellar inventory is meticulous — vintage, origin, and position on the rack. Some bottles have been here longer than the building's current owner.

Wine Storage.default: A curated environment for bottles of varying value and provenance. Wine storage is managed by catalog and trust. The catalog has been altered.

gender.male: The wine storage manifest shows a modification made to a recent inventory record. The original is still in the system.
gender.female: The wine storage catalog shows two different sets of entries for the same date. Someone edited the record after the fact.

Witness Waiting Area.default: A room where people wait. The waiting area is a space of rehearsed silence and unrehearsed anxiety.

gender.male: You move through the waiting area. Every face in here is carrying something. Patience runs thin under fluorescent lights.
gender.female: You sit in the waiting area. The people in here are restless. Waiting does things to people — it strips away the performance.

Women's Section.default: A designated space within a religious or commercial context. The women's section operates with its own internal norms and its own knowledge of what passes through it.

gender.male: You note the women's section entrance and identify the appropriate point of contact before proceeding.
gender.female: You enter the women's section with your badge and your awareness of the space. The community leader meets you respectfully and helps.

Woodshop.default: Sawdust and power tools, the smell of shavings and effort. The woodshop keeps records of what's been built. What it doesn't keep records of is what else has been done here.

gender.male: The woodshop is clean in the way a place gets cleaned when someone knows what to look for. You look for it anyway.
gender.female: You document the woodshop carefully. The sawdust patterns on the floor show recent use. The project board shows nothing scheduled.

X-Ray.default: Imaging that sees past the surface to what's underneath. You've found the X-ray to be a useful metaphor for everything you do.

gender.male: An after-hours X-ray at 11 PM has an authorisation record. Who signed off and who was on duty are both in the log.
gender.female: The X-ray machine is precise and clinical. It shows what's underneath. No opinions, no commentary — just the image.

X-Ray Equipment.default: Clinical imaging hardware for diagnostic assessment. Every activation is logged with a timestamp and an authorising name. The log is a precise record of who needed what and when.

gender.male: The X-ray activation log records every use with a timestamp and an authorising credential. Unscheduled activations appear as distinct entries in the log.
gender.female: The X-ray equipment usage records are available through the compliance officer. The records are produced and the results are examined.

X-Ray Room.default: A shielded space for diagnostic imaging. Access is controlled and every session logged. A room designed for seeing clearly — which is exactly what you intend to do.

gender.male: The X-ray room is cold and clinical. Lead-lined walls and the hum of a machine waiting for its next patient.
gender.female: You enter the X-ray room with the radiation safety officer. The lead shielding makes the room feel absolute. You ask your questions with the same quality.

Yacht Service.default: Marine maintenance and service for vessels that represent more capital than most people see in a lifetime. The yacht service knows every owner by name and every favor they've asked for.

gender.male: You walk the yacht service dock. The harbormaster has records on every vessel. One hasn't been accessed since it was reported missing from its slip.
gender.female: You speak with the yacht service manager. She maintains a meticulous service log. The vessel you're looking for was serviced not long ago. The owner arrived alone.

Yoga Studio.default: A room of controlled breath and deliberate movement. The yoga studio attracts people looking for stillness they can't find in the rest of their lives. You note what they're escaping.

gender.male: The yoga studio attendance system tracks member check-ins. A perfect attendance record that stopped abruptly is a notable pattern.
gender.female: The studio instructor's recall of her regulars extends to their last session and what was said before it.

Youth Center.default: A space built to give young people somewhere to be that isn't the street. The youth center sees the city's next generation before they've made their permanent choices.

gender.male: You sit down with the youth center director. He knows his regulars. He knows when something is wrong. Something has been wrong, and he's been waiting for someone to ask.
gender.female: The youth center is loud and warm. The program coordinator watches every kid who comes through the door. She watched yours.

Youth Lounge.default: An informal gathering space for adolescents — furniture, screens, and the social complexity of young people working out who they are.

gender.male: You wait in the youth lounge for the program supervisor. The teenagers give you a wide berth. That's fine. You're not here for them.
gender.female: The youth lounge coordinator knows her regulars by name and by trouble type. She introduces you to the one you need to speak with.

Youth Room.default: A dedicated room for young members of a community or institution. Whatever happened in here happened in a space designed to be safe.

gender.male: You enter the youth room after hours with the facility director. The room is undisturbed. Whatever happened here happened before Sunday.
gender.female: The youth room coordinator is protective of this space and its users. You respect that and ask your questions accordingly.
